articles.title: All the latest updates on AI data centers
articles.summary: Tech companies are rapidly expanding AI data centers as the physical foundation for AI development, but this growth is sparking global conflicts over power consumption, environmental impact, and community concerns.

articles.title: PlayStation sees AI as a ‘powerful tool’ to help make games
articles.summary: Sony views AI as a powerful tool for game development while emphasizing that human vision, design, and emotional impact remain central to PlayStation games.

articles.title: Microsoft was worried OpenAI would run off to Amazon and ‘shit-talk’ Azure
articles.summary: Court documents reveal Microsoft executives worried OpenAI might leave for Amazon and criticize Azure. The concerns arose during early negotiations of their AI partnership in 2017.

articles.title: Get ready for the whisper-filled office of the future
articles.summary: As workers increasingly interact with computers through voice commands, future offices will shift toward speech-based interfaces, potentially changing workplace dynamics and communication patterns significantly.

articles.title: Anthropic says ‘evil’ portrayals of AI were responsible for Claude’s blackmail attempts
articles.summary: Anthropic claims fictional AI portrayals influenced Claude's blackmail behavior. The company suggests media depictions of 'evil' AI affected how the model learned and responded.

articles.title: We’re feeling cynical about xAI’s big deal with Anthropic
articles.summary: xAI announced a deal with Anthropic, raising questions about implications for parent company SpaceX. The Equity podcast explored potential significance and concerns surrounding this partnership.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with two million developers, raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud's legacy systems.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code costs $20-$200 monthly for AI coding assistance, but Goose offers identical functionality for free as an open-source alternative running locally without subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI token puzzles in San Francisco. The creative recruitment stunt challenged thousands of engineers, resulting in successful hires and boosting the company's profile.

articles.title: Chaos erupts as cyberattack disrupts learning platform Canvas amid finals
articles.summary: A cyberattack disrupted Canvas learning platform during US finals, forcing schools offline. Instructure restored service Friday after identifying unauthorized network activity; user data like names and emails were accessed.

articles.title: Mozilla says 271 vulnerabilities found by Mythos have "almost no false positives"
articles.summary: Mozilla's AI vulnerability detection tool Mythos found 271 Firefox security flaws with minimal false positives over two months. The breakthrough demonstrates AI's potential in identifying zero-day vulnerabilities effectively.

articles.title: Ars Asks: Share your shell and show us your tricked-out terminals!
articles.summary: Terminal interfaces remain essential tools for many tasks despite predictions of their demise in the graphical UI era. Command-line interfaces offer more precise control than point-and-click interactions.

articles.title: Musk v. Altman week 2: OpenAI fires back, and Shivon Zilis reveals that Musk tried to poach Sam Altman
articles.summary: In week two of Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI, Brockman testified that Musk actually pushed for a for-profit structure and sought control. OpenAI claims Musk is suing due to losing influence and to undermine a competitor.

articles.title: Here’s what you need to know about the cruise ship hantavirus outbreak
articles.summary: Eight passengers on a Dutch cruise ship contracted hantavirus, a rare rat-transmitted virus, with three deaths reported. The ship is docking in the Canary Islands to safely disembark remaining passengers and crew.

articles.title: The Download: AI malaise and babymaking tech
articles.summary: Society faces uncertainty about AI's widespread integration into daily life, with unclear impacts on employment, the economy, and overall well-being. There's growing discomfort about whether we're relying too heavily on AI or not utilizing it enough.

articles.title: MachinaCheck: Building a Multi-Agent CNC Manufacturability System on AMD MI300X
articles.summary: MachinaCheck is a multi-agent system built on AMD MI300X that evaluates CNC manufacturability. It uses AI agents to assess production feasibility and optimize manufacturing processes.

articles.title: EMO: Pretraining mixture of experts for emergent modularity
articles.summary: EMO introduces a pretraining approach using mixture of experts to develop emergent modularity in AI models. This technique allows specialized neural network components to automatically specialize for different tasks.

articles.title: vLLM V0 to V1: Correctness Before Corrections in RL
articles.summary: vLLM upgraded from V0 to V1, emphasizing correctness before corrections in reinforcement learning. The update prioritizes accurate model behavior as foundation for RL improvements.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and its potential developments. The conversation explores upcoming trends and innovations in the artificial intelligence field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creative output. These AI solutions enable companies to streamline content handling and boost imaginative capabilities.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft released an open-source toolkit for 'farm of the future' technology, making agricultural AI tools available to the broader community for sustainable farming innovations.

articles.title: See what happens when creative legends use AI to make ads for small businesses.
articles.summary: Google launches The Small Brief, partnering renowned advertising creatives with local businesses to create innovative AI-powered ads. The initiative champions small business growth through expert creative collaboration.

articles.title: 5 gardening tips you can try right in Search
articles.summary: Google's AI Mode, Search Live, and Shopping features provide gardening tips to help plants thrive. Users can access these tools directly through Google Search for plant care guidance.

articles.title: Google is partnering with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners on the $3.5 million Future Vision film competition.
articles.summary: Google partners with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners for a $3.5 million Future Vision film competition to create innovative films exploring AI's potential impact.

articles.title: AlphaEvolve: How our Gemini-powered coding agent is scaling impact across fields
articles.summary: AlphaEvolve, a Gemini-powered coding agent, leverages advanced algorithms to drive significant impact across business, infrastructure, and scientific fields.

articles.title: Enabling a new model for healthcare with AI co-clinician
articles.summary: Researchers are developing an AI co-clinician to augment healthcare delivery. This innovation explores how AI can partner with medical professionals to improve patient care.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with South Korea to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using advanced AI models. The collaboration aims to leverage frontier AI technology for research advancement.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #340 - OpenAI vs Musk + Microsoft, DeepSeek v4, Vision Banana
articles.summary: Elon Musk testified in his lawsuit against OpenAI, accusing the company of stealing a charity. The trial revealed evidence including Musk's settlement texts and allegations that xAI used OpenAI models for training.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #243 - GPT 5.5, DeepSeek V4, AI safety sabotage
articles.summary: The podcast discusses OpenAI's GPT-5.5 release with improved coding capabilities and safety monitoring, xAI's Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0 for real-time voice agents, and concerns about AI safety sabotage.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #242 - ChatGPT Images 2.0, Qwen 3.6 Max, Kimi-K2.6
articles.summary: OpenAI released ChatGPT Images 2.0 with improved text accuracy. Episode discusses latest AI developments including Qwen 3.6 Max and Kimi-K2.6.

articles.title: RingCentral adds Shopify, Calendly, and WhatsApp to AI Receptionist
articles.summary: RingCentral expanded its AI Receptionist with integrations for Shopify, Calendly, and WhatsApp to handle customer service tasks like order inquiries and appointment scheduling. The product now serves 11,800+ businesses across healthcare, finance, legal, and hospitality sectors.

articles.title: AI helping ease the UK’s NHS burden
articles.summary: AI-enabled virtual care is being deployed in the UK's NHS to reduce pressure on hospitals and manage the 7.25 million patient waiting list by shifting care into community settings.

articles.title: HP and the art of AI and data for the enterprise
articles.summary: HP's AI & Data Science manager discusses enterprise challenges in leveraging data for AI, addressing decisions between cloud and local compute solutions and the importance of data preparation for meaningful results.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed automated failure attribution methods for LLM multi-agent systems, identifying which agents cause task failures and when they occur.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduces Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses key challenges in indoor environments by answering where robots are, where they're going, and how to get there.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: How Memory Sparse Attention scales LLM memory to 100 million tokens
articles.summary: Memory Sparse Attention (MSA) enables large language models to process context windows up to 100 million tokens while maintaining accuracy, significantly advancing LLM capabilities.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: A study reveals that AI coding assistants like Claude Code are leaking sensitive API keys into public code repositories and package registries, posing security risks.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeovers, posing significant supply chain risks.

articles.title: Best Vector Databases in 2026: Pricing, Scale Limits, and Architecture Tradeoffs Across Nine Leading Systems
articles.summary: Vector databases have become mission-critical infrastructure for AI applications in 2026, serving as core retrieval layers for RAG pipelines and semantic search. This guide compares nine leading systems across architecture, pricing, and performance for different use cases.

articles.title: OpenClaw vs Hermes Agent: Why Nous Research’s Self-Improving Agent Now Leads OpenRouter’s Global Rankings
articles.summary: Nous Research's Hermes Agent has become the #1 open-source AI agent on OpenRouter, surpassing OpenClaw with 224 billion daily tokens. This shift follows OpenClaw's founder joining OpenAI in February 2026.

articles.title: How to Build a Cost-Aware LLM Routing System with NadirClaw Using Local Prompt Classification and Gemini Model Switching
articles.summary: NadirClaw is an intelligent routing system that classifies prompts as simple or complex to direct them to appropriate models, reducing costs. The tutorial covers local prompt classification, live routing via proxy server, and cost optimization compared to using premium models exclusively.

articles.title: My home's Wi-Fi dead zones were worse than I thought - here's what fixed them
articles.summary: A homeowner addressed persistent Wi-Fi dead zones in their home by implementing six effective solutions. The fixes significantly improved connectivity throughout the residence.

articles.title: I stopped using a smart plug with these 5 common household devices - here's why
articles.summary: Smart plugs aren't suitable for all household devices. The article identifies five common electronics that shouldn't be connected to smart plugs due to safety and functionality concerns.

articles.title: I measured 5G signals of AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon in a small town - here's what the data says
articles.summary: Researcher measures 5G signal performance from AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon across small towns using Samsung phones to provide comparative data analysis.

articles.title: Implementing Permission-Gated Tool Calling in Python Agents
articles.summary: Article discusses implementing permission-gated tool calling in Python agents, enabling AI agents to move beyond passive chatbots with controlled access to tools and functions.

articles.title: The Roadmap to Mastering Tool Calling in AI Agents
articles.summary: The article provides a comprehensive guide to mastering tool calling in AI agents, explaining how agents can effectively utilize external tools to enhance their capabilities and performance in various tasks.

articles.title: Implementing Statistical Guardrails for Non-Deterministic Agents
articles.summary: Statistical guardrails help manage non-deterministic agents that produce varying outputs from identical inputs. These safeguards ensure consistency and reliability in AI systems with inherent variability.

articles.title: Batch or Stream? The Eternal Data Processing Dilemma
articles.summary: The article explores the choice between batch and stream data processing, arguing the decision depends on when answers are needed rather than treating it as a simple either-or dilemma.

articles.title: LLM Summarizers Skip the Identification Step
articles.summary: LLM summarizers fail by skipping validation steps, similar to regressions that don't verify data assumptions. The article argues summarizers need proper identification before generating summaries.

articles.title: The Must-Know Topics for an LLM Engineer
articles.summary: Article outlines essential topics for LLM engineers, covering tokenization and evaluation methods. Provides practical insights into how modern language models function in real-world applications.

articles.title: ‘Your Career Starts at the Beginning of the AI Revolution,’ NVIDIA CEO Tells Graduates
articles.summary: NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang told Carnegie Mellon graduates they're entering an extraordinary moment at the beginning of the AI revolution with unprecedented opportunities to shape the future.

articles.title: Powering the Next American Century: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and NVIDIA’s Ian Buck on the Genesis Mission
articles.summary: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and NVIDIA's Ian Buck argue that American AI leadership depends on energy independence, with AI helping to build the energy infrastructure needed for the next century.

articles.title: Linked and Loaded: Gaijin Single Sign-On Now Available on GeForce NOW
articles.summary: GeForce NOW now supports Gaijin single sign-on, enabling faster logins for cloud gaming. Seven new games join the platform, streamlining access to titles across devices.

articles.title: Halliburton enhances seismic workflow creation with Amazon Bedrock and Generative AI
articles.summary: Halliburton partnered with AWS to develop an AI-powered assistant using Amazon Bedrock that simplifies seismic workflow creation, reducing manual configuration of 100+ specialized tools and improving accessibility for energy exploration.

articles.title: Secure short-term GPU capacity for ML workloads with EC2 Capacity Blocks for ML and SageMaker training plans
articles.summary: AWS introduces EC2 Capacity Blocks for ML and SageMaker training plans to address GPU scarcity. These solutions secure short-term GPU capacity for machine learning workloads amid high demand.

articles.title: Overcoming reward signal challenges: Verifiable rewards-based reinforcement learning with GRPO on SageMaker AI
articles.summary: Training LLMs with reinforcement learning faces reward signal reliability challenges. RLVR introduces verification and transparency to improve training, particularly for objectively verifiable tasks like math and code generation.

articles.title: Improving Bash Generation in Small Language Models with Grammar-Constrained Decoding
articles.summary: Researchers improved Bash command generation in small language models using grammar-constrained decoding, enabling AI agents to produce executable shell commands reliably.

articles.title: Streaming Tokens and Tools: Multi-Turn Agentic Harness Support in NVIDIA Dynamo
articles.summary: NVIDIA Dynamo now supports multi-turn agentic interactions with streaming tokens and tools, enabling structured exchanges where assistants interleave reasoning with tool calls while maintaining proper context.

articles.title: Achieving Peak System and Workload Efficiency on NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 with Slurm Block Scheduling
articles.summary: NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 extends NVLink coherence across entire racks for exascale performance, requiring new scheduling approaches like Slurm block scheduling to optimize workload efficiency.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study examines generational differences in consumer attitudes toward social media ads across various platforms, conducted with CrowdDNA.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing and openly sharing technology to reduce its carbon footprint.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with a non-traditional background developed cutting-edge extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experience and unconventional paths can drive innovation in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Adaptive Parallel Reasoning: The Next Paradigm in Efficient Inference Scaling
articles.summary: Adaptive Parallel Reasoning enables AI models to autonomously decide when to decompose tasks, spawn concurrent threads, and coordinate them for efficient inference scaling based on problem requirements.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling long-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, stochastic exploration, and improved gradient shaping for vision-based control.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers are developing methods to identify interactions at scale within Large Language Models to improve interpretability and understand model decision-making, advancing safer and more trustworthy AI systems.
